description_long: This object is a pair of black, stylized knives with angular, geometric handles featuring large circular cutouts for finger placement. The blades are single-edged and slightly curved, tapering to a sharp point, and appear to be made from a matte, dark metal. The handles are thick and robust, with a knuckle-duster-like design that provides both grip and protection for the user's fingers. There are no visible guards separating the blade from the handle, giving the knives a modern, tactical appearance. The surface finish is mostly matte with some subtle highlights, suggesting a utilitarian, non-reflective coating. No decorative elements or ornamentation are present, emphasizing function over form.
description: A pair of black, angular knives with knuckle-duster-style handles and curved, single-edged blades. The design is modern, tactical, and utilitarian, with large finger holes and a matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both knives' curved blades and the prominent circular cutouts in the handles.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thinness of the blades and the flat profile of the handles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back edges of the handles and the alignment of the finger holes.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the depth of the handles and the overlapping arrangement of the knives.
attribute_handle_design: angular, thick, black metal with four large circular finger holes, knuckle-duster style
